summary: Recent updates in the image testing area of Firefox have focused on improving accuracy and compatibility for various image formats. Adjustments were made to the fuzziness tolerances when comparing images to ensure visual consistency, particularly when downscaling icons and dealing with new image formats like JXL (JPEG XL), which offers improved compression and quality. Additionally, support was added for Adobe-style extensions in image headers, enabling better handling of these resources. These enhancements are essential for maintaining display quality across different devices and ensuring that Firefox can properly interpret and process images from various sources, which ultimately contributes to a smoother user experience. **These updates are crucial as they enhance Firefox's capability to handle modern and efficient image formats, thereby improving performance and image fidelity.**
